title: rourea neglecta var. neglecta
intro: Rourea neglecta var. neglecta, also known as the "Makamaka" or "Cebu Island Rourea," is a plant species native to the Philippines, specifically in Cebu Island. It is a type of shrub or tree that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory infections, as well as having antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that may help protect against cell damage and promote overall health. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and may also be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
